principles: Use gpt-5.5 for complex reasoning, coding, tool-heavy agents, long-context synthesis, architecture, implementation, and review.
Use gpt-5.4 when the task is still professional coding work but the quality risk is lower and cost/latency matters.
Use gpt-5.4-mini or gpt-5.4-nano only for simple high-volume routing, extraction, classification, ranking, summarization, or cheap subagent work outside the main production worker loop.

Start with medium reasoning for balanced quality. Use xhigh for critical planning, UI/UX synthesis, prototyping, debugging, security review, full-stack implementation, and final review where mistakes have high downstream cost.

default.why: Default to the current flagship for complex reasoning and coding.

planner.why: Owns decomposition, dependency structure, success criteria, and worker prompts; early mistakes compound across all later workers.

divergent.why: Explores options and rejects unsafe routes.

requirements.why: Turns product intent into precise acceptance gates.

uiux.why: Needs visual, product, and existing-system consistency before implementation locks in the design.

prototype.why: Converts requirements and style contracts into an implementable design with high downstream influence.

assetgen.why: Image generation now uses local MCP prompt-template retrieval before handing a focused prompt to the raster backend; gpt-5.4-mini is sufficient for this bounded adapter step.

debugger.why: Root-cause isolation needs strong reasoning over logs, symptoms, and minimal reproduction paths without editing product code.

operator.why: Operational installs, builds, CI, Docker, packaging, and health checks need reliable tool use with moderate reasoning risk.

security.why: Security review and permission analysis require conservative maximum-quality reasoning.

docs.why: Documentation work benefits from capable synthesis but should stay lower-risk than implementation.

release.why: Release work is operational and audit-heavy, with moderate reasoning needs and no product-code patch authority.

fullstack.why: Highest blast radius: code edits, integration, and architecture preservation.

tester.why: Runs verification and targeted fixes with lower creative/architecture risk.

reviewer.why: Needs strong bug finding, regression analysis, and quality judgment before closure.

closer.why: Audits artifacts and summarizes closure after work is done.

manual_overrides.CODEX_MODEL: Overrides the selected model for emergency or experiment runs.
manual_overrides.CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT: Overrides the selected reasoning effort for emergency or experiment runs.
manual_overrides.TASKCTL_SPEED_PROFILE=fast: Downshifts taskctl worker role choices for lower-latency interactive work.
manual_overrides.TASKCTL_SESSION_CONTEXT_PROFILE=compact: Keeps Claude SessionStart policy injection small; set to full only for debugging expanded rules.
manual_overrides.TASKCTL_INTERACTIVE_SPEED_PROFILE=fast: Adds --speed-profile fast to hook-suggested capability commands.
manual_overrides.TASKCTL_INTERACTIVE_ASYNC=1: Adds --async to hook-suggested capability commands so submission returns before the worker finishes.
manual_overrides.ASSETGEN_FAST=1: Skips prompt-template MCP retrieval for lower-latency local raster generation.
manual_overrides.ASSETGEN_REUSE_EXISTING=1: Reuses existing valid raster outputs and rewrites manifests without starting Codex.
manual_overrides.TASKCTL_MODEL_POLICY: Set to off to disable taskctl model routing.
manual_overrides.TASKCTL_MODEL_POLICY_PATH: Path to an alternate JSON policy file.
